documentation:
# fromager

Fromager is a tool for completely re-building a dependency tree of
Python wheels from source.

The goals are to support guaranteeing

1. The [binary
   package](https://packaging.python.org/en/latest/glossary/#term-Built-Distribution)
   someone is installing was built from source in a known build
   environment compatible with their own environment
1. All of the package’s dependencies were also built from source -- any
   binary package installed will have been built from source
1. All of the build tools used to build these binary packages will
   also have been built from source
1. The build can be customized for the packager's needs, including
   patching out bugs, passing different compilation options to support
   build "variants", etc.

The basic design tenet is to automate everything with a default
behavior that works for most PEP-517 compatible packages, but support
overriding all of the actions for special cases, without encoding
those special cases directly into fromager.

## Using private registries

Fromager uses the [requests](https://requests.readthedocs.io) library and `pip`
at different points for talking to package registries. Both support
authenticating to remote servers in various ways. The simplest way to integrate
the authentication with fromager is to have a
[netrc](https://docs.python.org/3/library/netrc.html) file with a valid entry
for the host. The file will be read from `~/.netrc` by default. Another location
can be specified by setting the `NETRC` environment variable.

For example, to use a gitlab package registry, use a [personal
access
token](https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/profile/personal_access_tokens.html#create-a-personal-access-token)
as documented in [this
issue](https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/350582):

```plaintext
machine gitlab.com login oauth2 password $token
```

## Determining versions via GitHub tags

In some cases, the builder might have to use tags on GitHub to determine the version of a project instead of looking at
pypi.org. To avoid rate limit or to access private GitHub repository, a [personal access token](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens) can be passed to fromager by setting
the following environment variable:

```shell
GITHUB_TOKEN=<access_token>
```

## Additional docs

* [Using fromager](docs/using.md)
* [Package build customization instructions](docs/customization.md)
* [Developer instructions](docs/develop.md)

## What's with the name?

Python's name comes from Monty Python, the group of comedians. One of
their skits is about a cheese shop that has no cheese in stock. The
original Python Package Index (pypi.org) was called The Cheeseshop, in
part because it hosted metadata about packages but no actual
packages. The wheel file format was selected because cheese is
packaged in wheels. And
"[fromager](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/fromager)" is the French
word for someone who makes or sells cheese.

modules.name: fromager.tarballs
documentation: Based on https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/python-cryptography/blob/rawhide/f/vendor_rust.py

functions.name: fromager.tarballs._tar_reset
documentation: Reset user, group, mtime, and mode to create reproducible tar

functions.name: fromager.tarballs.tar_reproducible
documentation:
Create reproducible tar file

Add content from basedir to already opened tar. If prefix is provided, use
it to set relative paths for the content being added.

functions.name: fromager.external_commands.network_isolation_cmd
documentation:
Detect network isolation wrapper

Raises ValueError when network isolation is not supported
Returns: command list to run a process with network isolation

functions.name: fromager.external_commands.detect_network_isolation
documentation:
Detect if network isolation is available and working

unshare needs 'unshare' and 'clone' syscall. Docker's seccomp policy
blocks these syscalls. Podman's policy allows them.

functions.name: fromager.external_commands.run
documentation: Call the subprocess while logging output
based on pyproject_hooks/_impl.py: quiet_subprocess_runner

functions.name: fromager.overrides.get_versioned_patch_directories
documentation: This function will return directories that may contain patches for any version of a specific requirement.

functions.name: fromager.overrides.find_override_method
documentation:
Given a distname and method name, look for an override implementation of the method.

If there is no module or no method, return None.

If the module exists and cannot be imported, propagate the exception.

classes.name: fromager.clickext.ClickPath
documentation: ClickPath that returns pathlib.Path

classes.name: fromager.clickext.PackageVersion
documentation: Package version type that returns a packaging version

classes.name: fromager.clickext.RequirementType
documentation: RequirementType that returns a requirement type

functions.name: fromager.wheels._extra_metadata_elfdeps
documentation:
Analyze a wheel's ELF dependencies and add info files

Logs and returns library dependencies and library provides. Writes
requirements to dist-info.

functions.name: fromager.wheels._download_wheel_check
documentation: Helper method to check whether the .whl file is a zip file and has contents in it.
It will throw BadZipFile exception if any other file is encountered. Eg: index.html

functions.name: fromager.progress.progress
documentation: tqdm progress bar

functions.name: fromager.progress.progress_context
documentation: Context manager for progress bar with dynamic updates

functions.name: fromager.sources._download_source_check
documentation: Helper method to check whether .zip /.tar / .tgz is able to extract and check its content.
It will throw exception if any other file is encountered. Eg: index.html

functions.name: fromager.sources.default_prepare_source
documentation:
Unpack and modify sdist sources

Calls {func}`~fromager.sources.prepare_new_source` by default.

functions.name: fromager.sources.prepare_new_source
documentation:
Default steps for new sources

- patch sources
- apply project overrides from settings
- vendor Rust dependencies

{func}`~default_prepare_source` runs this function when the sources are new.

functions.name: fromager.sources.build_sdist
documentation: Build source distribution

functions.name: fromager.sources.pep517_build_sdist
documentation: Use the PEP 517 API to build a source distribution from a modified source tree.

methods.name: fromager.bootstrapper.Bootstrapper._explain
documentation: Return message formatting current version of why stack.

functions.name: fromager.finders._dist_name_to_filename
documentation:
Transform the dist name into a prefix for a filename.

Following https://peps.python.org/pep-0427/

functions.name: fromager.dependencies.default_get_build_system_dependencies
documentation:
Get build system requirements

Defaults to `[build-system] requires` from `pyproject.toml`.

functions.name: fromager.dependencies.default_get_build_backend_dependencies
documentation:
Get build backend dependencies

Defaults to result of hook call
{meth}`~pyproject_hooks.BuildBackendHookCaller.get_requires_for_build_wheel`

functions.name: fromager.dependencies.default_get_build_sdist_dependencies
documentation:
Get build sdist dependencies

Defaults to result of hook call
{meth}`~pyproject_hooks.BuildBackendHookCaller.get_requires_for_build_wheel`

modules.name: fromager.version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

functions.name: fromager.commands.lint.lint
documentation: Review existing settings and overrides for potential configuration errors.

modules.name: fromager.vendor_rust
documentation: Vendor Rust crates into an sdist

functions.name: fromager.vendor_rust._cargo_vendor
documentation: Run cargo vendor

functions.name: fromager.vendor_rust._cargo_shrink
documentation:
Remove pre-compiled archive and lib files.

They are only used on Windows, macOS, and iOS. This makes the vendor
bundle up to 60% smaller. If we ever need these files, then we have to
figure out how to compile them from sources, too.

functions.name: fromager.vendor_rust._cargo_config
documentation: create .cargo/config.toml

functions.name: fromager.vendor_rust._should_vendor_rust
documentation:
Detect if project has build requirement on Rust

Detects setuptools-rust and maturin.

functions.name: fromager.vendor_rust.vendor_rust
documentation:
Vendor Rust crates into a source directory

Returns `True` if the project has a build dependency on
`setuptools-rust` or `maturin`, and has a `Cargo.toml`, otherwise
`False`.

functions.name: fromager.commands.list_overrides.list_overrides
documentation: List all of the packages with overrides in the current configuration.

functions.name: fromager.commands.step.download_source_archive
documentation:
download the source code archive for one version of one package

DIST_NAME is the name of a distribution

DIST_VERSION is the version to process

SDIST_SERVER_URL is the URL for a PyPI-compatible package index hosting sdists

functions.name: fromager.commands.step.prepare_source
documentation:
ensure the source code is in a form ready for building a wheel

DIST_NAME is the name of a distribution

DIST_VERSION is the version to process

functions.name: fromager.commands.step.build_sdist
documentation:
build a new source distribution for the package

DIST_NAME is the name of a distribution

DIST_VERSION is the version to process

The source distribution is placed in the `sdists-repo/builds`
directory.

functions.name: fromager.commands.step.prepare_build
documentation:
set up build environment to build the package

DIST_NAME is the name of a distribution

DIST_VERSION is the version to process

functions.name: fromager.commands.step.build_wheel
documentation:
build a wheel from prepared source

DIST_NAME is the name of a distribution

DIST_VERSION is the version to process

functions.name: fromager.commands.build.build
documentation:
Build a single version of a single wheel

DIST_NAME is the name of a distribution

DIST_VERSION is the version to process

SDIST_SERVER_URL is the URL for a PyPI-compatible package index hosting sdists

1. Downloads the source distribution.

2. Unpacks it and prepares the source via patching, vendoring rust
   dependencies, etc.

3. Prepares a build environment with the build dependencies.

4. Builds the wheel.

Refer to the 'step' commands for scripting these stages
separately.

functions.name: fromager.commands.build.build_sequence
documentation:
Build a sequence of wheels in order

BUILD_ORDER_FILE is the build-order.json files to build

SDIST_SERVER_URL is the URL for a PyPI-compatible package index hosting sdists

Performs the equivalent of the 'build' command for each item in
the build order file.

variables.name: fromager.commands.build.build._fromager_show_build_settings
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: fromager.commands.build.build_sequence._fromager_show_build_settings
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: fromager.commands.bootstrap.bootstrap
documentation:
Compute and build the dependencies of a set of requirements recursively

TOPLEVEL is a requirements specification, including a package name
and optional version constraints.

variables.name: fromager.commands.bootstrap.bootstrap._fromager_show_build_settings
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: fromager.commands.canonicalize.canonicalize
documentation: convert a package name to its canonical form for use in override paths

functions.name: fromager.commands.migrate_config._parse_envfile
documentation: Parse a pkg.env file from Fromager 0.27

functions.name: fromager.commands.migrate_config._load_envfiles
documentation: Load default and variant env files from Fromager 0.27

functions.name: fromager.commands.migrate_config._migrate_package_envfiles
documentation:
Migrate settings.yaml of Fromager 0.27 and earlier

- "packages" section in setting.yaml
- "pre_built" section in settings.yaml
- separate env files in `env_dir` (<name>.yaml, <variant>/<name>.yaml)

functions.name: fromager.commands.migrate_config.migrate_config
documentation: Migrate Fromager 0.27 config to new format

functions.name: fromager.commands.build_order.as_csv
documentation:
Create a comma-separated-value file from the build order file

BUILD_ORDER_FILE is one or more build-order.json files to convert

Creates a file suitable for import into a spreadsheet including the
distribution name, version, original requirement, dependency type,
whether the package is pre-built, the build order step number, and
a full dependency chain leading to the requirment.

functions.name: fromager.commands.build_order.summary
documentation:
Summarize the build order files

BUILD_ORDER_FILE is one or more build-order.json files to convert

Creates a comma-separated-value file including the distribution
name, the build order file that included it, which versions are
used in each, and where they match.

functions.name: fromager.commands.download_sequence.download_sequence
documentation:
Download a sequence of source distributions in order.

BUILD_ORDER_FILE is the build-order.json files to build

SDIST_SERVER_URL is the URL for a PyPI-compatible package index hosting sdists

Performs the equivalent of the 'step download-source-archive' command only for items in
the build order file that have source_url_type as sdist.

functions.name: fromager.packagesettings._before_builddirectory
documentation: build directory

functions.name: fromager.packagesettings._validate_envkey
documentation: Validate env key, converts int, float, bool
environment variables map

functions.name: fromager.packagesettings.substitute_template
documentation: Substitute ${var} and ${var:-default} in value string

functions.name: fromager.packagesettings.get_cpu_count
documentation: CPU count from scheduler affinity

functions.name: fromager.packagesettings.get_available_memory_gib
documentation: available virtual memory in GiB

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageVersion
documentation: Pydantic-aware package version
version

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.ResolverDist

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.ResolverDist.sdist_server_url
documentation: Source distribution download server (default: PyPI)

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.ResolverDist.include_sdists
documentation: Use sdists to resolve? (default: yes)

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.ResolverDist.include_wheels
documentation: Use wheels to resolve? (default: no)

Packages resolver dist

```
sdist_server_url: https://pypi.org/simple/
include_sdists: True
include_wheels: False
```

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.DownloadSource

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.DownloadSource.url
documentation: Source download url (string template)

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.DownloadSource.destination_filename
documentation: Rename file (filename without path)

Package download source

Download package sources from an alternative source, e.g. GitHub release.

```
url: https://example.com/package.tar.gz
destination_filename: ${dist_name}-${version}.tar.gz
```

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.BuildOptions

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.BuildOptions.build_ext_parallel
documentation:
Configure `build_ext[parallel]` in `DIST_EXTRA_CONFIG`

This enables parallel builds of setuptools extensions. Incompatible
with some packages, e.g. numba 0.60.0.

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.BuildOptions.cpu_cores_per_job
documentation:
Scale parallel jobs by available CPU cores

Examples:

1: as many parallel jobs as CPU logical cores

2: allocate 2 cores per job

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.BuildOptions.memory_per_job_gb
documentation:
Scale parallel jobs by available virtual memory (without swap)

Examples:

0.5: assume each parallel job requires 512 MB virtual memory

Build system options

```
build_ext_parallel: False
cpu_cores_per_job: 1
memory_per_job_gb: 1.0
```

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.ProjectOverride

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.ProjectOverride.update_build_requires
documentation: Add / update requirements to pyproject.toml `[build-system] requires`

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.ProjectOverride.remove_build_requires
documentation: Remove requirement from pyproject.toml `[build-system] requires`

Override pyproject.toml settings

```
update_build_requires:
  - setuptools
remove_build_requires:
  - ninja
```

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.VariantInfo

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.VariantInfo.env
documentation: Additional env vars (overrides package env vars)

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.VariantInfo.wheel_server_url
documentation: Alternative package index for pre-built wheel

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.VariantInfo.pre_built
documentation: Use pre-built wheel from index server?

Variant information for a package

```
env:
  VAR1: "value 1"
  VAR2: "2.0
wheel_server_url: https://pypi.org/simple/
pre_build: False
```

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.from_mapping
documentation: Load from a dict

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.from_string
documentation: Load from raw yaml string

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.from_file
documentation:
Load from file

Raises {exc}`FileNotFound` when the file is not found.
The package name is taken from the stem of the file name.

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.from_default
documentation: Create a default package setting

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.override_module_name
documentation: Override module name from package name

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.serialize
documentation: Serialize package configuration

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.name
documentation: Canonicalized package name

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.has_config
documentation: package has override setting

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.build_dir
documentation: sub-directory with setup.py or pyproject.toml

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.changelog
documentation: Changelog entries

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.env
documentation: Common env var for all variants

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.download_source
documentation: Alternative source download settings

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.resolver_dist
documentation: Resolve distribution version

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.build_options
documentation: Build system options

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.project_override
documentation: Patch project settings

class_variables.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageSettings.variants
documentation: Variant configuration

Package settings

```
build_dir: python
changelog:
    "1.0.1":
        - fixed bug
env:
    EGG: spam
download_source:
    url: https://egg.test
    destination_filename: new_filename
resolver_dist:
    sdist_server_url: https://sdist.test/egg
    include_sdists: true
    include_wheels: false
variants:
    cpu:
        env:
            EGG: spamalot
        wheel_server_url: https://wheel.test/simple
    rocm:
        pre_built: True
```

classes.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.package
documentation: Package name

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.variant
documentation: Variant name

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.plugin
documentation: Get Fromager plugin module

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.get_all_patches
documentation: Get a mapping of version to list of patches

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.get_patches
documentation: Get patches for a version (and unversioned patches)

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.has_config
documentation: Does the package have a config file?

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.pre_built
documentation: Does the variant use pre-build wheels?

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.wheel_server_url
documentation: Alternative package index for pre-build wheel

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.override_module_name
documentation: Override module name from package name

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.download_source_url
documentation: sdist download URL

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.download_source_destination_filename
documentation: Rename sdist to dest filename

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.resolver_sdist_server_url
documentation: Package index server URL for resolving package versions

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.resolver_include_wheels
documentation: Include wheels when resolving package versions?

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.resolver_include_sdists
documentation: Include sdists when resolving package versions?

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.build_dir
documentation: Build directory for package (e.g. subdirectory)

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.build_tag
documentation: Build tag for version's changelog and this variant

methods.name: fromager.packagesettings.PackageBuildInfo.get_extra_environ
documentation:
Get extra environment variables for a variant

1. parallel jobs: `MAKEFLAGS`, `MAX_JOBS`, `CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL`
2. PATH and VIRTUAL_ENV from `build_env` (if given)
3. package's env settings
4. package variant's env settings

`template_env` defaults to `os.environ`.

modules.name: fromager.extras_provider
documentation:
A provider that handles packages with "extras".

Python package dependencies can include "extras", which are additional
dependencies that are installed "on demand". For instance, project X could
have an additional set of dependencies if PDF generation features are needed.
These can be defined for an extra "pdf" and requested on install as X[pdf].

The basic resolvelib algorithm cannot handle extras, as it builds a dependency
graph which needs to be static - the edges (dependencies) from a node
(candidate) must be fixed. Extras break this assumption.

To model projects with extras, we define a candidate as being a project with a
specific set of dependencies. This introduces a problem, as the resolver could
produce a solution that demands version 1.0 of X[foo] and version 2.0 of
X[bar]. This is impossible, as there is actually only one project X to be
installed. To address this, we inject an additional dependency for every
candidate with an extra - X[foo] version v depends on X version v. By doing
this, we constrain the solution to require a unique version of X.

classes.name: fromager.extras_provider.ExtrasProvider

methods.name: fromager.extras_provider.ExtrasProvider.get_extras_for
documentation:
Given a requirement or candidate, return its extras.

The extras should be a hashable value.

methods.name: fromager.extras_provider.ExtrasProvider.get_base_requirement
documentation:
Given a candidate, return a requirement that specifies that
project/version.
A provider that handles extras.

modules.name: fromager.__main__

methods.name: fromager.build_environment.BuildEnvironment.python
documentation: Path to Python interpreter in virtual env

methods.name: fromager.build_environment.BuildEnvironment.get_venv_environ
documentation:
Add virtual env to extra environ

The method activates the virtualenv

1. Put the build environment at the front of the {envvar}`PATH`
   to ensure any build tools are picked up from there and not global
   versions. If the caller has already set a path, start there.
2. Set {envvar}`VIRTUAL_ENV` so tools looking for that (for example,
   maturin) find it.

documentation:
Run command in a virtual environment

`network_isolation` defaults to context setting.

documentation: Write pyproject.toml to build directory

methods.name: fromager.pyproject.PyprojectFix._default_build_system
documentation: Add / fix basic 'build-system' dict

Auto-fixer for pyproject.toml settings

- add missing pyproject.toml
- add or update `[build-system] requires`

Requirements in `update_build_requires` are added to
`[build-system] requires`. If a requirement name matches an existing
name, then the requirement is replaced.

Requirements in `remove_build_requires` are removed from
`[build-system] requires`.
